title: Recess Duty: Watching William's Playground Empire

sentences.en: Friday. Since William's teacher was out sick, I got asked to walk him to the playground during my own free period, which is how I ended up on recess duty.
sentences.zh: 星期五。因為 William 的老師請病假，我被叫去在我自己的空堂時間帶他去操場，這就是我怎麼變成負責顧他下課時間的原因。

sentences.en: The playground was already full of younger kids racing down the narrow path toward the swings before anyone else could claim them first.
sentences.zh: 操場上已經擠滿了低年級小朋友，大家沿著那條窄窄的小路狂奔，搶著要在別人之前先佔到鞦韆。

sentences.en: William sprinted straight for a whole row of swings, picked the one exactly in the middle, and declared it "his throne for the day."
sentences.zh: William 直接衝向整排鞦韆，選了正中間那一個，宣布這是「他今天的王座」。

sentences.en: A group of girls nearby were playing a clapping game, hands moving so fast it looked less like a game and more like a tiny competition of reflexes.
sentences.zh: 附近一群女生在玩拍手遊戲，手速快到與其說是遊戲，更像是一場小小的反應力競賽。

sentences.en: William hopped off the swing after barely a minute, insisting the slide needed his attention more urgently.
sentences.zh: William 才盪不到一分鐘就跳下鞦韆，堅持溜滑梯更需要他的關注。

sentences.en: The slide's surface was smooth and slightly too fast, sending William flying off the end and directly into a small pile of sand.
sentences.zh: 溜滑梯的滑道又滑又有點太快，把 William 一路送出去，直接飛進一小堆沙子裡。

sentences.en: He immediately stood up, brushed off the sand, and announced this had been "exactly according to plan," which nobody believed for a second.
sentences.zh: 他馬上站起來，拍掉身上的沙子，宣布這一切「完全照計畫進行」，沒有人相信這句話半分。

sentences.en: Nearby, two older students were completely focused on a game of chess, ignoring the chaos around them like true masters of concentration.
sentences.zh: 附近兩個高年級學生完全專注在一盤棋上，無視周遭的一片混亂，展現出真正的專注力大師風範。

sentences.en: A younger girl worked quietly on a puzzle at a picnic table, carefully fitting one stubborn piece that clearly did not want to cooperate.
sentences.zh: 一個小女孩在野餐桌上安靜地拼著拼圖，小心翼翼地想擠進一片很不合作的頑固拼圖塊。

sentences.en: William, always looking for one more thing to do, tried to play a trick on his friend by hiding behind the seesaw and jumping out suddenly.
sentences.zh: William 總是在找下一件事做，他想對朋友惡作劇，躲在蹺蹺板後面，突然跳出來嚇人。

sentences.en: The trick worked a little too well, and his friend jumped so hard in surprise that the seesaw flew up and nearly bonked William on the chin.
sentences.zh: 這個惡作劇效果好過頭了，他朋友嚇得跳起來，力道大到蹺蹺板整個彈起來，差點打到 William 的下巴。

sentences.en: Before recess ended, a teacher handed out crayons for a quick outdoor art activity, and William drew what he insisted was a dragon, though it looked mostly like a potato with wings.
sentences.zh: 下課結束前，一位老師發了蠟筆，讓大家做一個快速的戶外美術活動，William 畫的東西他堅持是一條龍，但看起來比較像一顆長了翅膀的馬鈴薯。

sentences.en: I told him to stretch his artistic imagination a little further next time, and he took that as a genuine compliment.
sentences.zh: 我跟他說，下次可以再多發揮一點藝術想像力，他把這句話當成了真心的稱讚。

sentences.en: When the bell rang, everyone had to hurry back to their classrooms, William included, still holding his slightly sandy dragon-potato drawing proudly.
sentences.zh: 鐘聲一響，每個人都得趕緊跑回教室，William 也不例外，還驕傲地拿著他那張帶點沙子的龍馬鈴薯畫作。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: recess duty is exhausting, but watching William run his own tiny playground empire for twenty minutes was strangely entertaining.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：顧下課雖然很累，但看 William 在短短二十分鐘裡經營他自己那個小小的操場王國，意外地滿有娛樂性的。

quiz.question: What happens when William plays a trick behind the seesaw?
options: His friend jumps so hard the seesaw nearly hits William's chin.
He falls off the seesaw laughing.
Nobody notices the trick at all.

quiz.question: What are the two older students doing while ignoring the chaos around them?
What does William draw during the outdoor art activity?
options: A dragon that looks mostly like a potato with wings.
A picture of the playground itself.
